steps.enzyme_name: Pterocarpan reductase / cyclization enzyme (CYP-mediated ring closure)

heterologous_genes.function: Phenylalanine ammonia-lyase; deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate, the first committed step in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Trans-cinnamate 4-monooxygenase; hydroxylates cinnamate to 4-coumarate. CYP450 requiring CPR electron donor
4-Coumarate:CoA ligase; activates coumarate to coumaroyl-CoA using ATP
Chalcone synthase; condenses coumaroyl-CoA with 3 malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone. Works with CHR for isoliquiritigenin
Chalcone reductase; co-acts with CHS to produce isoliquiritigenin (6'-deoxychalcone) instead of naringenin chalcone
Chalcone isomerase; cyclizes isoliquiritigenin to liquiritigenin (a flavanone)
2-Hydroxyisoflavanone synthase; catalyzes hydroxylation and 1,2-aryl migration converting liquiritigenin to 2,7,4'-trihydroxyisoflavanone. Key CYP450 for isoflavone skeleton
2-Hydroxyisoflavanone dehydratase; dehydrates trihydroxyisoflavanone to produce daidzein
Isoflavone 2'-hydroxylase; hydroxylates daidzein to 2'-hydroxydaidzein. CYP450 requiring CPR
Isoflavone reductase; NADPH-dependent reduction of 2'-hydroxydaidzein to (3R)-vestitone
Glycinol 4-dimethylallyltransferase; prenylates the pterocarpan/isoflavan intermediate using DMAPP to install the dimethylallyl group needed for glabridin
NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase; essential electron transfer partner for all three CYP450 enzymes (C4H, IFS/CYP93C2, I2'H/CYP81E1)

knockouts.rationale: Blocks Ehrlich pathway degradation of phenylpyruvate to phenylacetaldehyde, redirecting flux toward L-phenylalanine synthesis for the heterologous pathway

overexpressions.rationale: Rate-limiting entry point into the shikimate pathway; feedback-resistant mutant ARO4-K229L eliminates tyrosine inhibition, boosting phenylalanine precursor supply
Directs chorismate toward prephenate and the phenylalanine branch; feedback-resistant mutant ARO7-G141S removes feedback inhibition by tyrosine
Major NADPH-producing reaction; pathway requires 6 NADPH per glabridin molecule. Overexpression increases cytoplasmic NADPH supply via the pentose phosphate pathway

expression_risks: IFS/CYP93C2 (Q9SXS3): 1 transmembrane helix (aa 2-22) anchors to ER membrane; 59.4 kDa; instability index 45.1 (borderline unstable). Heme-dependent CYP450 requiring co-expression with CPR.
CYP81E1/I2'H (P93147): Membrane-associated CYP450 (57.2 kDa); heme-dependent. No annotated TM helices but localized to membrane. Requires CPR co-expression.
C4H/CYP73A5 (P92994): ER membrane-anchored CYP450 requiring CPR. Well-characterized in yeast heterologous expression.

G4DT (B9A1Q4): 9 transmembrane helices (highly hydrophobic, GRAVY +0.308); 46.0 kDa; native localization to chloroplast membrane. Major expression risk in yeast: may need N-terminal signal removal and ER targeting. Consider truncation of chloroplast transit peptide region.
ATR2/CPR (Q9SUM3): 1 TM helix (aa 52-72); 78.9 kDa; ER membrane-anchored. Well-characterized for yeast expression. Requires FAD and FMN cofactors.
recommended_mutations: ARO4-K229L: Feedback-resistant DAHP synthase; removes allosteric inhibition by L-tyrosine, increasing flux into the shikimate pathway (well-established in yeast metabolic engineering)
ARO7-G141S: Feedback-resistant chorismate mutase; removes allosteric inhibition by L-tyrosine, directing more flux toward prephenate/phenylalanine

IFS/CYP93C2: S310 and K375 are critical for aryl migration activity. AVOID mutations at these positions (S310A/T and K375A/T cause loss of isoflavanone synthase activity). R104 is essential (R104A = total loss).
ACC1-S659A/S1157A: Phosphorylation-resistant acetyl-CoA carboxylase; prevents Snf1-mediated inactivation, maintaining malonyl-CoA supply under glucose-limited conditions
coexpression_requirements: ATR2 (CPR) MUST be co-expressed with all three CYP450 enzymes (C4H, IFS/CYP93C2, CYP81E1/I2'H). ATR2 from A. thaliana (Q9SUM3) is the recommended reductase partner, validated for coupling with CYP73A5.
CHS7 and CHR must be co-expressed simultaneously to produce isoliquiritigenin (6'-deoxychalcone). CHS alone produces naringenin chalcone, not the desired isoliquiritigenin.
Consider INO2/INO4 overexpression or ERG9 downregulation for ER membrane expansion if CYP450 competition for ER space limits activity.
protein_engineering_notes.notes: The pathway contains 3 CYP450 enzymes (C4H, IFS, I2'H) all competing for ER membrane space and CPR electron transfer. This is a major bottleneck. Recommendations: (1) Use multi-copy CPR integration (2:1 CPR:CYP ratio recommended). (2) Consider ERG9 promoter replacement with HXT1p (glucose-repressed) to reduce sterol synthesis and free ER membrane. (3) G4DT has 9 TM helices and is natively chloroplast-localized; re-targeting to ER using an ER signal anchor or testing as a truncated soluble variant is critical. (4) The DMAPP prenyl donor is supplied via the native mevalonate pathway; consider tHMG1 (truncated HMG-CoA reductase) overexpression to boost IPP/DMAPP supply. (5) Heme supply may become limiting with 3 CYP450s; consider HEM3 overexpression.

fba_results.strategy_description: Heterologous 11-step pathway from L-phenylalanine to glabridin added to iMM904. Baseline theoretical maximum flux is 0.635 mmol/gDW/h (no biomass constraint) or 0.574 mmol/gDW/h (10% min biomass). Optimized strategy: ARO10 knockout (blocks Ehrlich pathway phenylpyruvate degradation) + ARO4 overexpression (min flux 0.5, boosts shikimate entry) + ARO7 overexpression (min flux 0.3, boosts chorismate-to-prephenate) + ZWF1 overexpression (min flux 2.0, boosts NADPH supply via pentose phosphate pathway). Optimized flux: 0.568 mmol/gDW/h at 10% biomass (0.024 gDW/h). The pathway is primarily limited by NADPH (6 mol/mol product), malonyl-CoA (3 mol/mol), DMAPP (1 mol/mol), and O2 supply (3 mol/mol).
